You can use the following one-liner to return only a JSON array of questions:
```javascript
Object.keys(obj).map(question => question);
This will give you an array containing all the keys from the `obj` object, which in your case is `questions`. Here's how it works:
`Object.keys()` returns an array of property names (keys) in the given object.
`map()` applies a provided function to each element of an array and returns a new array with the results.
In this case, we're mapping over the `obj` object (`questions`) and assigning each key-value pair as a separate string.
